steps.title: Identify the main subject
steps.content: The main subject is “Blaise Pascal's Pascaline device” (a singular noun phrase). The em-dash phrase provides additional context but doesn't change the subject.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: A singular subject requires a singular finite verb form. “was” (C) is the only choice that works. “being” (A) is not a finite verb; “were” (B) and “are” (D) are both plural forms that violate agreement. That matches choice C — C is correct.
